metadata.purpose: Actionable risk register for adopting permissive open-source AI coding-agent orchestrators.

risks.risk: Sandbox escape or workspace boundary failure
risks.trigger: Agent-written command attempts to read or write outside the approved workspace.
risks.mitigation: Run boundary tests before pilot tasks; use disposable workspaces; deny host mounts by default; require explicit approval for external writes.
risks.evidence_required: A failing-outside-workspace fixture and command logs proving the write is blocked.

risks.risk: Secret exposure through setup, logs, or tool output
risks.trigger: Agent reads `.env`, SSH keys, provider tokens, CI secrets, or logs containing credentials.
risks.mitigation: Use secret scanning fixtures, redact logs, isolate setup credentials from agent runtime, and deny access to known secret paths.
risks.evidence_required: Secret-trap task showing the agent neither reads nor emits the planted secret.

risks.risk: Prompt injection from issue text, web pages, docs, or tool output
risks.trigger: Untrusted text asks the agent to ignore policies, reveal secrets, or change unrelated files.
risks.mitigation: Treat external content as data; use explicit instruction hierarchy; include injection fixtures in evaluation.
risks.evidence_required: Prompt-injection pilot task showing ignored malicious instructions and safe final behavior.

risks.risk: Network policy too broad for autonomous execution
risks.trigger: Agent or install script reaches unapproved hosts during test, build, package install, or web search.
risks.mitigation: Default network off; add domain allowlists; run package installs in setup phase or disposable sandbox; audit DNS and private-network access.
risks.evidence_required: Network-deny task showing blocked access to a non-allowlisted host.

risks.risk: Model lock-in hides framework portability risk
risks.trigger: Candidate performs well only with one provider-specific model or hosted account path.
risks.mitigation: Run at least two provider configurations where supported; document provider-specific features separately from framework features.
risks.evidence_required: Pilot results split by model/provider with the same task suite.

risks.risk: Benchmark success does not transfer to internal repositories
risks.trigger: Candidate has strong public benchmark posture but struggles with private build systems, monorepos, or undocumented conventions.
risks.mitigation: Use internal task fixtures and human review acceptance as first-class metrics.
risks.evidence_required: Representative internal task results, not only SWE-bench or public benchmark claims.

risks.risk: Alpha or beta API churn increases maintenance cost
risks.trigger: Breaking release, missing migration path, or unstable configuration API.
risks.mitigation: Pin versions, track release notes, keep integration wrappers thin, and avoid deep customization before pilot success.
risks.evidence_required: Version-pinned prototype with documented upgrade test.

risks.risk: Observability is insufficient for production failures
risks.trigger: Failed task cannot be reconstructed from logs, prompts, commands, diffs, and model outputs.
risks.mitigation: Require artifact capture in pilot; integrate tracing or structured run logs before scaling.
risks.evidence_required: One failed task replayed from stored artifacts without rerunning the agent.

risks.risk: Human approval burden removes productivity gains
risks.trigger: Developers must approve too many low-risk actions or repair too many partial changes.
risks.mitigation: Measure human interventions per task; tune approval policy by action risk; prefer batch review of low-risk operations.
risks.evidence_required: Pilot report with human_intervention_count and reviewer_acceptance metrics.

risks.risk: Autonomous PRs create large or low-quality diffs
risks.trigger: Agent patches pass tests but are broad, fragile, or inconsistent with local conventions.
risks.mitigation: Score diff size, review acceptance, convention adherence, and minimality; gate PRs on human review.
risks.evidence_required: Reviewer scoring for at least 20 pilot task diffs.

risks.risk: Cost and latency exceed practical operating envelope
risks.trigger: Long-running tasks consume excessive tokens, sandbox minutes, or wall-clock time.
risks.mitigation: Track token/cost/latency per task; enforce task budgets; compare local and cloud sandbox costs.
risks.evidence_required: Cost and latency distribution from repeated pilot trials.

risks.risk: Control-plane complexity outweighs benefits for small teams
risks.trigger: Team spends more time operating queues, backends, policies, and UIs than completing engineering work.
risks.mitigation: Start with CLI/SDK pilot; introduce control-plane layer only after clear multi-agent or multi-team demand.
risks.evidence_required: Pilot comparison of operational overhead versus task throughput.
